title: World Optometry Day 2026
shortDescription: World Optometry Day 2026 at Guru Nanak College featured an insightful session by Dr. Pooja Sharma, along with student presentations, poster displays, a quiz competition, and cultural performances.
fullDescription: World Optometry Day 2026 at Guru Nanak College was celebrated with an engaging and insightful session led by Dr. Pooja Sharma, HoD Optometry at Amritsar Eye Clinic and Fellow in Contact Lens from LV Prasad Eye Institute, Hyderabad, with over 10 years of experience. The event featured student presentations, poster showcases, an interactive quiz competition, and vibrant cultural performances, making it a perfect blend of learning and celebration.

title: Vidya Abhyaas–III
shortDescription: Vidya Abhyaas-III kicks off! Our students are trading classrooms for clinics this week (Feb 2-7) for hands-on clinical exposure at the hospital. A full week of practical learning, real patient interactions, and transforming theory into practice. This is where knowledge meets reality! Watch them grow from students to future healthcare professionals.
fullDescription:
Vidya Abhyaas–III is a structured clinical exposure program designed to provide students with practical, real-world healthcare experience. Conducted from February 2 to February 7, the program enables students to transition from classroom-based learning to hands-on clinical training in a hospital environment.

During this one-week program, students actively participate in supervised clinical activities, observe medical procedures, and engage in real patient interactions. The initiative focuses on strengthening clinical skills, improving professional competence, and enhancing understanding of hospital protocols and patient care practices.

Under the guidance of experienced healthcare professionals, students apply theoretical concepts to practical situations, helping them develop confidence, ethical awareness, and decision-making abilities essential for the healthcare profession.
Vidya Abhyaas–III plays a vital role in shaping students into competent and responsible future healthcare professionals by bridging the gap between academic knowledge and clinical practice.

title: Lohri Celebration
shortDescription: Industry-focused learning for future professionals.
fullDescription:
Guru Nanak College, Dehradun celebrated the vibrant festival of Lohri, marking the harvest season and the spirit of new beginnings. The celebration reflected gratitude for nature’s abundance and highlighted the cultural significance of Lohri as a festival of warmth, prosperity, and joy.

The event brought students and faculty together in a festive atmosphere filled with traditional music, folk dances, and the symbolic lighting of the bonfire. Participants celebrated the occasion with enthusiasm, expressing thankfulness for prosperity and embracing the values of unity and togetherness.

The Lohri celebration at Guru Nanak College promoted cultural awareness and community bonding, offering students an opportunity to experience and appreciate India’s rich traditions. The event concluded with renewed positivity, collective joy, and a shared hope for growth, happiness, and success in the year ahead.

title: Free Medical Camp
shortDescription: SJS Joshi Multi Speciality Hospital proudly served the Rampur Kala community through our Free Health Camp! Our dedicated team conducted comprehensive health screenings, bringing quality healthcare to your doorstep.
fullDescription:
Guru Nanak College, Dehradun, in collaboration with SJS Joshi Multi Speciality Hospital, successfully organized a Free Health Camp for the residents of Rampur Kala as part of its ongoing commitment to community welfare and healthcare outreach.

The camp aimed to make quality healthcare services accessible at the community level. A team of experienced medical professionals conducted comprehensive health screenings, offered medical consultations, and provided guidance on preventive healthcare and healthy lifestyle practices. The initiative focused on early detection of health issues and raising awareness about the importance of regular health check-ups.

The event witnessed active participation from the local community, reflecting trust and engagement in the healthcare services provided. Through this initiative, the institution reinforced its dedication to social responsibility and the well-being of society.

The Free Health Camp contributed towards building a healthier community by promoting awareness, accessibility, and compassionate care—taking a meaningful step towards a healthier tomorrow.

title: World's Aids Day
shortDescription: World AIDS Day - December 1st. Together, we can make a difference in the fight against AIDS. Take a stand, lend a hand, and help create awareness. Let’s unite for a healthier, more compassionate world.
fullDescription:
Guru Nanak College, Dehradun observed World AIDS Day on December 1st with the objective of spreading awareness, promoting compassion, and strengthening collective efforts in the fight against HIV/AIDS. The event emphasized the importance of education, prevention, and support for individuals and communities affected by the disease.

The program focused on creating awareness about the causes, prevention methods, and myths associated with HIV/AIDS through informative sessions and discussions. Students were encouraged to take an active role in advocating for empathy, inclusivity, and responsible health practices, helping to reduce stigma and discrimination.

Through this observance, Guru Nanak College reaffirmed its commitment to public health awareness and social responsibility. The event served as a platform to inspire unity, encourage informed action, and promote a healthier and more compassionate society.

title: Vidya Abhyas-II
shortDescription: Vidya Abhyas - II kicks off today at Guru Nanak College, Jhajjar, Dehradun!This week, we’re trading textbooks for hands-on experiences, classrooms for labs, and theory for REAL-WORLD practice! Our students across all disciplines are diving into pure practical learning.
fullDescription:
Guru Nanak College, Jhajjar, Dehradun successfully organized Vidya Abhyas–II, a week-long experiential learning program aimed at strengthening practical knowledge and skill-based education across all disciplines.

The program was designed to shift the learning approach from traditional classroom instruction to hands-on practical exposure. During the week, students actively engaged in laboratory work, practical sessions, demonstrations, and activity-based learning under the guidance of experienced faculty members.

Vidya Abhyas–II emphasized the philosophy of “Learning by Doing”, enabling students to apply theoretical concepts to real-world situations. The initiative encouraged critical thinking, problem-solving, and technical competence, helping students gain confidence in their respective fields of study.

Through this immersive learning experience, Guru Nanak College reinforced its commitment to outcome-based education and holistic student development, preparing learners to meet academic and professional challenges with practical expertise.

title: Inter College Sports Tournament
shortDescription: A high-energy sports meet bringing together talent from various colleges.
fullDescription:
The Inter College Sports Tournament at Guru Nanak College is a premier athletic event that fosters sportsmanship and competitive spirit among students from various institutions. The campus transformed into a vibrant arena as athletes competed in disciplines like cricket, football, volleyball, and athletics.

The tournament serves as a platform for young athletes to showcase their physical prowess, teamwork, and dedication. Beyond the competitions, the event highlights the importance of fitness and a healthy lifestyle, encouraging students to balance academic excellence with athletic achievements. It was a day of intense games, cheering crowds, and celebrated victories.

title: Workshop on Disaster Risk & Management
shortDescription: Equipping students with essential life-saving skills and emergency protocols.
fullDescription:
The Workshop on Disaster Risk & Management was organized to prepare students and faculty for unforeseen emergencies. The session covered critical aspects of disaster preparedness, including risk assessment, emergency response protocols, and recovery strategies.

Experts in disaster management shared valuable insights on handling natural and man-made disasters, emphasizing the importance of quick decision-making and efficient resource allocation. Participants engaged in mock drills and practical demonstrations, ensuring they are better equipped to handle real-life crises and contribute to community safety.

title: BLS Workshop
shortDescription: Hands-on Basic Life Support (BLS) training for emergency situations.
fullDescription:
The Basic Life Support (BLS) Workshop provided intensive training on life-saving techniques, including CPR and AED usage. Healthcare professionals guided students through the critical steps of providing immediate assistance to individuals experiencing cardiac arrest or choking.

The workshop emphasized the 'Golden Hour' in emergency medicine and the role of timely intervention in saving lives. Students practiced on mannequins, gaining the confidence and skills necessary to remain calm and effective in high-pressure medical emergencies.

title: Nasha Mukti Awareness Program
shortDescription: Promoting a drug-free campus and community through education and advocacy.
fullDescription:
The Nasha Mukti (Drug-Free) Awareness Program was a proactive initiative to educate students about the dangers of substance abuse. The event featured expert lectures, interactive sessions, and impactful presentations on the physical and psychological consequences of addiction.

The program aimed to empower students to make informed choices and become advocates for a healthy, drug-free lifestyle. By fostering a supportive and aware environment, the college reaffirmed its commitment to the holistic well-being of its students and the broader society.

title: Industry Exposure
shortDescription: Bridging the gap between academic theory and real-world industrial practices.
fullDescription:
The Industry Exposure visit provided students with a first-hand look at the operations of a leading manufacturing facility. Guided tours through various departments allowed students to witness theoretical concepts being applied in large-scale industrial processes.

Interactions with industry professionals offered insights into current market trends, technological advancements, and the workforce skills required in the modern world. This experience proved invaluable in helping students align their academic goals with their future career aspirations.

title: Children's Day Carnival
shortDescription: Celebrating the spirit of childhood with joy, games, and cultural festivities.
fullDescription:
The Children's Day Carnival was a vibrant celebration dedicated to the joy and innocence of childhood. The campus was filled with laughter as students participated in various fun activities, food stalls, and cultural performances.

The event also highlighted the importance of nurturing young minds and providing them with a platform to express their creativity and talents. Faculty and students came together to make the day memorable, reaffirming the college's role in the holistic development and happiness of its community members.

title: Faculty Development Programme
shortDescription: Empowering educators through innovative teaching methodologies and academic research.
fullDescription:
The Faculty Development Programme (FDP) was designed to enhance the professional competencies of the teaching staff. The sessions focused on modern pedagogical tools, advanced research methodologies, and effective classroom management strategies.

Prominent academicians shared their expertise, encouraging faculty members to adopt innovative approaches that foster a deeper understanding of subjects among students. The program provided a platform for knowledge exchange and professional growth, ensuring the institution continues to provide high-quality education.

title: World Radiology Day
shortDescription: Recognizing the vital role of medical imaging in modern healthcare and diagnosis.
fullDescription:
World Radiology Day was observed to acknowledge the contribution of radiologists and radiographers in patient care. The event featured technical sessions on the latest advancements in medical imaging technologies, including MRI, CT, and ultrasound.

The celebration also included discussions on the safety protocols and ethical considerations in radiology. Students of Radiology and Medical Imaging participated in various competitions, showcasing their technical knowledge and reaffirming the importance of accurate diagnosis in successful medical treatment.

title: Guru Nanak Jayanti
shortDescription: Celebrating the birth and teachings of the first Sikh Guru, Guru Nanak Dev Ji.
fullDescription:
Guru Nanak Jayanti was celebrated with profound reverence and spiritual fervor. The campus resonated with the messages of universal brotherhood, peace, and service to humanity, which are the core teachings of Guru Nanak Dev Ji.

The celebration included a solemn prayer session, Kirtan, and Guru ka Langar, where students and faculty members from all backgrounds participated together. The event served as a reminder of the timeless values of equality and compassion, inspiring the college community to lead a life of purpose and integrity.

title: Celebrating the 150th Birth Anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
image: upload/gnc-events/Celebrating the 150th Birth Anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel/image-1.webp
shortDescription: Commemorating the 150th birth anniversary of the Iron Man of India and his role in national integration.
fullDescription:
The 150th birth anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was celebrated with patriotic pride. The event honored the visionary leadership and indomitable spirit of the Iron Man of India, who played a pivotal role in the unification of the nation.

The program included an 'Unity Run-for-Run' and various competitions focusing on the life and contributions of Sardar Patel. Students were inspired to uphold the values of national unity and integrity, reflecting on the lessons from the history of India's independence and integration.

title: World Anesthesia Day
shortDescription: Celebrating the impact of anesthesia on surgery and patient comfort.
fullDescription:
World Anesthesia Day was celebrated to highlight the critical role of anesthesia in modern medical procedures. The event focused on the advancements in anesthetic practices, patient safety protocols, and the evolving role of the anesthesiologist in the surgical team.

Students of Operation Theatre Technology and Anesthesia participated in informative sessions and simulations, gaining a deeper understanding of the technical and empathetic aspects of their profession. The celebration served as a tribute to the pioneers of anesthesia and a call to continue striving for excellence in patient care.

title: Rangrezza 2025
shortDescription: The annual cultural extravaganza celebrating art, music, dance, and youth talent.
fullDescription:
Rangrezza 2025 was a two-day cultural festival that transformed the campus into a kaleidoscope of colors and creativity. The mega event featured a diverse range of competitions, including group dances, solo singing, fashion shows, and street plays (Nukkad Natak).

The festival provided a platform for students to express their artistic talents and celebrate the spirit of youth. With electrifying performances and a festive atmosphere, Rangrezza brought together the entire college community, fostering a sense of unity and pride. It was a celebration of cultural diversity and the vibrant energy that defines Guru Nanak College.

title: Plantation Drive
shortDescription: A green initiative to promote environmental sustainability and ecological balance.
fullDescription:
The Plantation Drive was organized as part of the college's 'Go Green' initiative, aiming to contribute to environmental conservation. Students and faculty members came together to plant various saplings across the campus and neighboring areas.

The event emphasized the importance of trees in maintaining ecological balance and combating climate change. Educational sessions on environmental protection and sustainable living were held, encouraging participants to adopt eco-friendly practices in their daily lives. The drive was a small but significant step towards a greener and healthier future.

title: Dusshera Celebration
shortDescription: Celebrating the victory of good over evil with traditional fervor and joy.
fullDescription:
The Dusshera celebration at Guru Nanak College was a festive occasion marked by traditional rituals and cultural performances. The event symbolized the victory of virtue and truth, resonating with the timeless message of the Ramayana.

Students participated in folk dances and plays depicting the life of Lord Rama, while the symbolic burning of the effigy of Ravana served as a reminder of the triumph of good over evil. The celebration fostered a spirit of unity and cultural appreciation among the diverse student body, bringing people together in a joyeous atmosphere.

title: Jagran Night
shortDescription: A night of spiritual awakening and devotional music dedicated to the Divine.
fullDescription:
Jagran Night was an evening of deep spiritual connect and devotional fervor. The campus resonated with soul-stirring Bhajans and hymns, creating a serene and uplifting atmosphere for everyone present.

The event brought together students, faculty, and their families in a collective expression of faith and gratitude. The night was not just about music but also about fostering community bonds and providing a peaceful space for reflection and spiritual growth. It was an experience that left a lasting impact on the hearts of all participants.

title: Dandhiya Night
shortDescription: Vibrant music, traditional attire, and rhythmic dance to celebrate the spirit of Navratri.
fullDescription:
Dandhiya Night was a colorful and energetic celebration that brought the festive spirit of Navratri to life on campus. Students dressed in traditional ethnic wear participated with enthusiasm, dancing to the rhythmic beats of folk music.

The event was a wonderful showcase of India's cultural richness and the collective joy of our students. Beyond the dance, it was an opportunity for students to bond, celebrate diversity, and create lasting memories in a lively and inclusive environment.

title: World Pharmacy Day
shortDescription: Recognizing the vital role of pharmacists in improving global healthcare and patient safety.
fullDescription:
World Pharmacy Day was celebrated with a series of activities highlighting the evolving role of pharmacists in the healthcare system. The theme focused on 'Pharmacists Strengthening Health Systems', emphasizing their contribution to medicine safety and patient care.

The School of Pharmacy organized seminars, technical quizzes, and professional networking sessions. Students engaged with industry experts, gaining insights into the latest pharmaceutical advancements and the ethical responsibilities of their profession. The celebration reinforced the college's commitment to developing competent and compassionate pharmacists.

title: Blood Donation Camp
shortDescription: A humanitarian initiative to save lives through voluntary blood donation.
fullDescription:
The Blood Donation Camp was organized to support the local blood banks and promote the spirit of social responsibility. Students, faculty, and staff participated in large numbers, demonstrating their commitment to saving lives and serving the community.

The event was conducted under the supervision of medical experts, ensuring all safety and hygiene protocols were strictly followed. Participants were educated on the health benefits of regular blood donation, fostering a sense of pride and accomplishment in contributing to a noble cause. It was a day that highlighted the college's dedication to humanitarian values.

title: Vidya Abhyas- I
shortDescription: The inaugural practical learning week focusing on hands-on training and skill development.
fullDescription:
Vidya Abhyas-I kicked off the academic year's series of experiential learning programs. This week-long initiative focused on bridging the gap between theoretical concepts and their practical applications.

Through guided lab sessions, workshop demonstrations, and activity-based learning, students across various departments gained valuable hands-on experience. The program emphasized the importance of technical proficiency and problem-solving skills, laying a strong foundation for the students' academic and professional journeys ahead.

title: SEBI Awareness Program
shortDescription: Educating students on financial literacy, investment safety, and regulatory frameworks.
fullDescription:
The SEBI Awareness Program was a specialized initiative to enhance financial literacy among students. Experts from the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) shared insights on the capital markets, investment avenues, and the importance of investor protection.

The session covered practical topics such as market analysis, safe investment practices, and the role of SEBI in regulating the financial sector. Students engaged in interactive discussions, gaining a clearer understanding of the financial landscape and the importance of making informed financial decisions in their personal and professional lives.

title: IQAC Workshop
shortDescription: Ensuring quality assurance and excellence in academic and administrative processes.
fullDescription:
The internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) organized a workshop focusing on the continuous improvement of academic standards. The sessions addressed key aspects of quality management, including curriculum design, assessment methodologies, and student feedback mechanisms.

Faculty members collaborated on developing strategies to achieve institutional excellence and meet global academic benchmarks. The workshop emphasized the institution's commitment to providing a superior learning environment and fostering a culture of accountability and innovation in all academic and administrative activities.

title: World Physiotherapy Day
shortDescription: Advocating for the importance of physiotherapy in rehabilitation and holistic health.
fullDescription:
World Physiotherapy Day was observed to recognize the vital contribution of physiotherapists in improving physical health and quality of life. The event featured workshops on advanced clinical techniques, physical rehabilitation strategies, and sports injury management.

The School of Physiotherapy organized awareness camps and interactive sessions, highlighting the role of movement and exercise in health maintenance. Students showcased their skills through demonstrations and case presentations, emphasizing the science and empathy that underpin the physiotherapy profession. It was a day dedicated to the theme of 'Enhancing Mobility and Independence'.

title: Jashn-E-Guru Teacher's Day Celebration
shortDescription: Honoring our educators for their dedication, guidance, and profound influence on our lives.
fullDescription:
Jashn-E-Guru was a heartfelt celebration dedicated to the faculty members of Guru Nanak College on Teacher's Day. Students organized a vibrant cultural program, including performances, speeches, and interactive games, to express their gratitude for their mentors.

The event highlighted the noble role of teachers in shaping the future of students and the importance of the guru-shishya relationship. It was a day of joy and mutual respect, where the entire college community came together to celebrate the art of teaching and the spirit of learning.

title: Ganesh Chaturthi
shortDescription: Welcoming Lord Ganesha with devotion, joy, and cultural festivities on campus.
fullDescription:
Ganesh Chaturthi was celebrated on the college campus with profound spiritual fervor and traditional gaiety. The installation of the Ganesha idol was followed by rituals, devotional singing, and cultural performances by students.

The festival brought together people from all walks of life, celebrating the message of wisdom, prosperity, and the removal of obstacles. The serene and joyous atmosphere fostered community bonding and provided a spiritual experience that resonated with the values of the institution.

title: Vidya Shakti
shortDescription: Empowering students through specialized skill development and personality enrichment workshops.
fullDescription:
Vidya Shakti was a week-long enrichment program designed to empower students with the skills and confidence needed for a successful future. The sessions focused on soft skills, communication, leadership, and personal branding.

Through interactive workshops and mentorship from industry experts, students were encouraged to explore their potential and develop a growth mindset. The program also included sessions on mental well-being and life skills, ensuring a holistic approach to student development beyond the academic curriculum.

title: National Sports Day
shortDescription: Celebrating the spirit of sportsmanship and fitness on the birth anniversary of Major Dhyan Chand.
fullDescription:
National Sports Day was observed with various athletic competitions and awareness sessions about the importance of physical fitness. The event paid tribute to the legendary hockey player Major Dhyan Chand, inspiring students to pursue sports with passion and discipline.

The day-long activities included friendly matches, fitness challenges, and discussions on the role of sports in character building. The college community participated with immense enthusiasm, reaffirming their commitment to a healthy and active lifestyle.

title: Free Healthcare Checkup Camp
shortDescription: Providing essential medical screenings and health consultations to the local community.
fullDescription:
A series of free healthcare checkup camps were organized to provide medical support and awareness to the neighboring areas. A team of specialist doctors and healthcare staff conducted screenings for various common ailments and provided free medical consultations.

The initiative targeted the underserved populations, offering them access to quality healthcare at no cost. The camp also focused on raising awareness about preventive health measures, hygiene, and nutrition, contributing to the overall well-being of the community.

title: World Photography Day
shortDescription: Capturing life's moments and celebrating the art of visual storytelling.
fullDescription:
World Photography Day was celebrated with a campus-wide photography competition and workshop. The event aimed to inspire students to see the world through a creative lens and develop their skill in visual communication.

The workshops covered both technical and artistic aspects of photography, ranging from camera settings to composition and post-processing. A photo exhibition showcased the incredible talent and unique perspectives of our students, reaffirming photography's power to document history and evoke emotions.

title: Janamasthmi 2025
shortDescription: Celebrating the birth of Lord Krishna with devotional fervor, music, and Dahi Handi.
fullDescription:
Janamasthmi was celebrated with immense joy and traditional festivities. The campus was decorated with colorful flowers and lights, creating a spiritually vibrant atmosphere. The celebration included the traditional 'Dahi Handi' competition, where students exhibited team spirit and resilience.

The event featured devotional songs, dance performances depicting the life of Lord Krishna, and a collective prayer session. It was a day of cultural enrichment and communal celebration, bringing together the institutional family in a festive bond.

title: Independence Day
shortDescription: Honoring our nation's freedom and the sacrifices of our heroes with pride and patriotism.
fullDescription:
Independence Day was celebrated with patriotic fervor, beginning with the hoisting of the national flag. The event served as a solemn reminder of the struggle for independence and the responsibility we carry as citizens of a free nation.

The program included patriotic songs, speeches by faculty and students, and various cultural activities that showcased India's rich heritage and diversity. The celebration inspired the youth to contribute meaningfully to the progress and unity of our great nation.

title: Anti-ragging week Awareness Program
shortDescription: Ensuring a safe and inclusive campus environment through education and anti-ragging advocacy.
fullDescription:
The Anti-ragging awareness week was a series of activities aimed at creating a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment for all students. The program emphasized the institution's 'zero-tolerance' policy towards ragging and the legal consequences of such actions.

The week featured sensitization workshops, legal awareness sessions, and interactive programs that encouraged students to respect one another and report any grievances. By fostering a culture of mutual support and safety, the institution reaffirmed its commitment to the well-being and dignity of every student.

title: Pajama Party
shortDescription: A fun-filled informal gathering for newcomers to break the ice and build friendships.
fullDescription:
The Pajama Party was a unique and informal ice-breaking event organized for the new batch of students. Designed to be a relaxed and fun-filled evening, it provided a platform for freshers to bond with their peers and seniors.

The event included music, games, and informal storytelling, creating a welcoming and comfortable atmosphere for everyone. This gathering played a significant role in helping students settle into campus life and feel a sense of belonging within the institutional community.

title: Vidya Aarambh
shortDescription: Welcoming the new batch with a comprehensive induction program into college life.
fullDescription:
Vidya Aarambh was the induction and orientation program for the incoming batch of students. The week-long program aimed to familiarize students with the college's culture, academic requirements, and various co-curricular opportunities.

Through interactive sessions with faculty, seniors, and alumni, the program provided a clear understanding of the institutional vision and the students' role in their own success. The initiative focused on creating a supportive foundation for the new learners, ensuring they are well-prepared for their academic and personal journey at Guru Nanak College.
